summary: Crystal‑clear 1967 soundboard capture of a blistering show highlighted by Viola Lee Blues, Alligator → Caution and a ripping Jerry Garcia.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard source – crisp, balanced and virtually free of noise aside from a minor glitch in Viola Lee Blues. Musically the performance is legendary: the band rockets off the opening Viola Lee Blues into a soaring Alligator‑Caution climax, with standout moments from Good Morning Little Schoolgirl, Hurts Me Too and an unforgettable Other One.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance (standout jams, tight playing) and the technical quality (crystal‑clear mix). Minor glitches are noted but do not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear, well‑balanced mix; occasional hiss expected of vintage tape and a reported glitch around 8:30 in Viola Lee Blues, but otherwise pristine.

poor_songs: Other One (some reviewers felt this version was not among the very best)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show launches with a bluesy Viola Lee Blues, builds through Pigpen’s vocal rap and Good Morning Little Schoolgirl, then rolls into an epic Alligator‑Caution suite that serves as the high‑water mark of the evening; pacing stays energetic throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his fierce Les Paul tone, ripping solos on Viola Lee Blues and Other One, and overall guitar dominance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is described as clear, walking and duetting with Jerry on Morning Dew; drummers’ interplay also highlighted his groove.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ/harp work gets strong compliments; no specific mention of Keith or Brent.
band_member_comments.Drums: Dual‑drummer chemistry (Billy & Mickey) noted as complementary and powerful yet well‑mixed.
